summary: Iteration 53 - Security P3 login hardening verified end-to-end. All 11 backend tests passed (100%). Driver/shipper/admin logins still succeed and tokens work on protected routes. Rate limiting (5 failed attempts -> 6th = HTTP 429 'Too many failed attempts') confirmed on all three login endpoints (/api/auth/login, /api/auth/shipper-login, /api/auth/admin-login) using throwaway emails. Rate-limit isolation works (locking email A does not lock email B). Successful login resets the throttle counter. Suspended-account block returns HTTP 403 on /api/auth/login; demo driver was reactivated and confirmed to log in (200). CORS sanity: API reachable, login with Origin header works, wildcard ACAO is advertised WITHOUT credentials.
critical_code_review_comments: Throttle is in-memory per process (auth.py L20). Already documented in code comment. For multi-worker prod use Redis. Acceptable for current single-worker setup.
Successful admin login resets throttle by email (auth.py L128). Correct since the throttle key is the lowercased email.
CORS middleware (server.py L4277-4288) correctly disables allow_credentials when wildcard is used; safe since auth uses Bearer tokens, not cookies.
seed_data_creation: Used existing demo accounts via POST /api/seed-demo (idempotent). All bruteforce tests used unique throwaway emails (bruteforce-*-<uuid>@nadaruns.com). Demo driver was suspended and reactivated within the same test; final state verified as 200 OK login.
context_for_next_testing_agent: P3 login security verified at iteration 53. Demo accounts are NOT locked - throttle is per-email in-memory and only throwaway emails were brute-forced. Demo driver was suspended+reactivated and confirmed logging in at end of suite. Re-run: pytest backend/tests/test_iter53_login_security.py -v
rca_of_issue: N/A - no bugs found. Feature works as specified.
